channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Vice-governador de SP, Felício Ramuth troca PSD pelo MDB e cita alinhamento com Tarcísio
item.description: São Paulo's Vice Governor Felício Ramuth announced his departure from the PSD and affiliation with the MDB, citing alignment with Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' political project. He thanked the PSD and highlighted MDB leadership — including Ricardo Nunes, Baleia Rossi, and Michel Temer — as an environment conducive to contributing to São Paulo and Brazil's development.

item.title: Vice de Tarcísio decide se filiar ao MDB após rompimento com Kassab
item.description: São Paulo's vice-governor, Felício Ramuth, has decided to join the MDB following a split with PSD national president Gilberto Kassab, who sought the vice-presidential spot on Tarcísio de Freitas' 2026 re-election ticket. Ramuth stated his decision aligns with the governor's project and came after a conversation with São Paulo mayor Ricardo Nunes (MDB), whose approval was required for the move. The vice vacancy is also being contested by Kassab and the PL, which had proposed André do Prado.

item.title: De olho no 2º turno, Zema testa fôlego contra Flávio
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo) has launched a national tour to consolidate his presidential candidacy, banking on his efficiency record and completed infrastructure projects in Minas Gerais. Despite right-wing fragmentation and strong competition from Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), Zema holds 61% approval in Minas according to Paraná Pesquisas, while his vice governor Mateus Simões (PSD) assumes state leadership.

item.title: Lula aparece à frente de Flávio Bolsonaro entre eleitores de centro, diz Datafolha
item.description: Lula leads Flávio Bolsonaro among center voters (self-identified as 4 on a 1-7 scale) with 31% to 17% in the first round and 41% to 32% in a potential runoff, according to Datafolha. Both candidates are technically tied in the overall electorate and among unaligned voters (position 3 on a 1-5 scale). The margin of error is five percentage points.

item.title: Defesa pede a Moraes 'livre acesso' de filhos à casa onde Bolsonaro cumpre prisão domiciliar
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro has requested Minister Alexandre de Moraes of the STF to revise visitation conditions for the former president's non-resident children, who currently face restricted access to his house arrest residence. Lawyers argue the restriction creates unequal treatment between children living in the home and those who do not, and seek 'free access' compatible with existing security measures. Bolsonaro is serving a 90-day house arrest after leaving DF Star Hospital due to health reasons, following a 27-year-and-3-month sentence for coup attempt.

item.title: “Blindagem absoluta da base do governo”
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected the final report by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), which recommended indicting over 200 individuals, including politicians, former ministers, and Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha. The report was overturned by 19 votes against in a decision made early Saturday, March 28. The move is seen as a sign of absolute shielding by the government’s base against investigations targeting Lula’s allies.

item.title: Datafolha: Lula supera Flávio Bolsonaro entre eleitores de centro
item.description: Lula leads among centrist voters in the 2026 electoral scenario, surpassing Flávio Bolsonaro according to Datafolha poll. The survey shows the PT president has an advantage among those identifying as center (position 4 on a 1 to 7 scale) in one of the tested scenarios. The result highlights Lula's ability to attract support beyond his traditional left-wing base.

item.title: Tebet chama Nunes de 'deselegante' e ‘agressivo’ por dizer que ela é 'marionete do Lula': 'se eu fosse homem não teria coragem'
item.description: Planning Minister Simone Tebet (PSB) criticized São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes (MDB) for calling her a 'Lula puppet', calling the remark aggressive and disrespectful, particularly toward Brazilian women. Tebet stated she does not take orders from men and said Nunes' comment reinforces sexist stereotypes that discourage women from politics. Despite the conflict, she said she still holds great affection for Nunes, her 2022 ally.

item.title: Com novos ajustes, governo vai turbinar Minha Casa, Minha Vida e o setor imobiliário
item.description: The federal government announced new adjustments to the Minha Casa, Minha Vida program to align income brackets with the increased minimum wage, keeping bracket 1 equivalent to two minimum wages (R$ 3.200). The proposal, to be voted by the FGTS Curatorial Council on March 24, aims to expand homeownership access, boost real estate launches, and sustain profitability for builders, which already report margins above historical averages. The changes, occurring in an election year, reflect a shift in government posture from periodic maintenance to frequent updates to prevent program obsolescence.

item.title: Lula à frente de Flávio entre eleitores de centro, segundo Datafolha
item.description: Lula (PT) leads Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) among center-leaning voters, according to a Datafolha poll conducted March 3–5. The survey tested first-round scenarios without Ratinho Junior, who withdrew his candidacy. Lula appears ahead in this key electoral segment.

item.title: Como Tarcísio e Haddad se preparam para duelo paulista de temperatura elevada
item.description: The 2026 São Paulo gubernatorial election is shaping up to mirror national polarization between petism and bolsonarism, with Fernando Haddad (PT) and Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans) as the main contenders. Tarcísio leads in polls, benefiting from his incumbent governor status, while Haddad targets perceived weaknesses such as free-flow toll roads, the privatization of Sabesp, and the imbalance favoring federal over state infrastructure projects in the state.

item.title: O imposto de exportação
item.description: Lula zeroed PIS/Cofins taxes on diesel until December 2026 to counter international oil price hikes, fuel inflation, and declining popularity, mirroring a 2022 Bolsonaro measure but without the forced ICMS reduction that required state compensation. To offset lost revenue, he imposed a 12% export tax (IE) on petroleum exports, reviving a tool historically used to curb domestic inflation by diverting goods to the internal market — now seen as economically irrational and driven primarily by electoral objectives.

item.title: Roberto Reis na Crusoé: Teremos outsider em 2026?
item.description: The article questions whether there is room for an outsider candidate in the 2026 Brazilian elections, given the dominance of Lula and Bolsonaro. It notes that the absence of Ratinho Jr., who was polling in third place, creates a vacuum that could be filled by a new figure, who is quietly growing while the two main poles focus on their rivalry.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: veja como cada parlamentar votou na análise que rejeitou relatório
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected, by a vote of 19 to 12, the report proposed by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (União Brasil-AL) in the early hours of Saturday (28). The rejection was supported by members of the government base and the Centrão, while favorable votes came from the opposition to President Lula’s government. The outcome reflects partisan divisions in the investigation of alleged irregularities at the National Institute of Social Security.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleição indireta e mantém governador interino no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for the government of Rio de Janeiro and maintained Rio de Janeiro State Court President Ricardo Couto de Castro as interim governor. The decision also removed the case from the virtual environment and brought it to the Court's plenary for deliberation.

item.title: Cristiano Zanin suspende eleições indiretas para mandato-tampão no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended indirect elections for the governorship of Rio de Janeiro pending plenary review, ordering TJ-RJ President Ricardo Couto to remain in office. The ruling grants a PSD petition challenging the validity of indirect elections after Claudio Castro's resignation and also suspends a preliminary injunction by Justice Luiz Fux on the matter. Zanin had previously voted for direct elections, asserting Castro's resignation was a strategic move to avoid disqualification and keep PL allies in power.

item.title: ‘Congelamento’ de Messias deixa STF desfalcado e expõe tensão entre Lula e Congresso
item.description: The freezing of Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF by President Lula reflects the fragility of the government's congressional base and the fear of an institutional defeat in an election year. The attempt to accelerate the hearing by Senator Davi Alcolumbre was annulled under government pressure, leaving the Court with one justice missing for nearly five months. Although Messias was announced in November 2025 to appeal to evangelical voters, his formal nomination remains suspended, exposing the tension between the Executive and Legislative branches over judicial appointments.

item.title: Janja vira Marisa e Dilma vira Irma Passoni em falas de Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, aged 80, has been making memory lapses in public speeches, such as calling Janja Marisa and Dilma Irma Passoni. Since 2023, at least 157 episodes of controversial remarks or forgetfulness have been recorded, including comments on pet spending, an inverted meaning in a speech on organized crime, and a geographic error about Brazil’s borders. These incidents occur as Lula seeks to demonstrate physical vigor ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleição indireta para governo do Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for the governor of Rio de Janeiro, granting a request from the PSD to hold a direct vote instead. The decision keeps Justice Ricardo Couto de Castro as interim governor until the Court rules definitively, with a hearing set for March 31. Former governor Cláudio Castro (PL), who resigned to run for Senate, was declared ineligible by the TSE for abuse of power in the 2022 elections, barring him from running until 2030.

item.title: O telefonema de Janja e uma senadora do Centrão após o PL da Misoginia
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva spoke by phone with Senator Soraya Thronicke (Podemos-MS), the rapporteur of the Misogyny Bill, to discuss the approval of the project criminalizing misogynistic discourse. The bill, unanimously approved by the Federal Senate, provides for prison sentences of up to five years and will now proceed to the Chamber of Deputies. Janja posted a video on social media urging swift approval, stating the project is vital to protect women's lives.

item.title: Agressões, disputas eleitorais e guerra virtual marcaram CPMI do INSS
item.description: The INSS CPI, officially concluded on March 28, 2025, was marked by intense political polarization, featuring physical altercations, verbal confrontations, and repeated disruptions of testimonies between government and opposition lawmakers. Initially tasked with investigating associative discount frauds and possible corruption schemes, the committee devolved into a battleground of competing narratives, especially after the approval of breaking fiscal and banking secrecy for Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), the president’s son, prompting retaliatory moves such as an 800-page alternative report by government allies.

item.title: CPMI do INSS termina sem relatório aprovado, após 7 meses de trabalho
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Commission (CPMI) concluded after seven months of work without approving a final report, following the rejection of rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar’s (União-AL) text by a vote of 19 to 12. The report sought the indictment of 216 individuals, including Fabio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula, for alleged involvement in the billion-dollar misappropriation of pensions and retirement benefits. Despite efforts to extend the commission’s mandate and a favorable ruling by Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça, the full Court reversed the decision, ruling the matter falls under internal congressional authority.

item.title: Por que a CPMI do INSS acabou em pizza, na avaliação de bolsonaristas
item.description: Bolsonarist leaders attribute the failure to approve the final report of the INSS CPMI to the Master scandal, which they claim diverted the commission's focus and caused the Centrão to shift support to the Lula government, rejecting Deputy Alfredo Gaspar's report. The CPMI ended without an approved report after a 19-12 vote against Gaspar's text, with government allies attempting to submit an alternative report that was not accepted by the committee president.

item.title: Vídeo: CPMI do INSS: relatório é rejeitado por 19 votos a 12
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected the final report by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (União-AL) after a 17-hour meeting, with 19 votes against and 12 in favor. The report was deemed inadequate by committee members.

item.title: Áudio: CPMI do INSS termina com rejeição do relatório final
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected, by a vote of 19 to 12, in the early hours of Saturday (28), the final report presented by federal deputy Alfredo Gaspar (União-AL), which proposed the indictment of 216 individuals involved in billion-real frauds targeting retirees and pensioners’ benefits. Installed in August 2023, the committee concluded its work without issuing an official final report after the rejection.

item.title: Viana chama de "blindagem" a rejeição do relatório final da CPMI do INSS
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana, president of the INSS CPI, condemned the rejection of the final report as a 'shield' for the government, claiming it ignored evidence of social security fraud. Despite the 18-12 vote against the report, he said he fulfilled the mission entrusted by the Brazilian people. The original report sought indictments against 216 individuals, including Lula’s son, over alleged embezzlement of billions in pension funds.

item.title: CPI do INSS termina como palco de disputa política e falta de relatório vai virar mote de campanha
item.description: The INSS CPI concluded without approving a final report after government allies rejected the draft by deputy Alfredo Gaspar, enabling the opposition to claim the Lula administration shielded the investigation involving the president’s son, while government supporters argue their version reflected the majority and excluded other implicated parties. The commission, meant to investigate pension fraud and mismanagement, devolved into a political battleground, with mutual accusations between Lula and Bolsonaro factions and a failed attempt to implicate STF ministers linked to banker Daniel Vorcaro. The outcome fuels electoral narratives for 2026, as both sides rapidly produce social media content to sway voters.

item.title: Governistas vão levar relatório paralelo à PF mesmo sem aprovação na CPMI
item.description: Deputy Paulo Pimenta (PT-RS) called it an affront to democracy that the president of the INSS CPMI, Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), refused to put the parallel report to a vote. The official report was rejected by 18 votes to 12, ending the CPMI’s work without a final document. Despite this, government supporters said they will submit the parallel report — which blames the Bolsonaro administration for the INSS fraud and names 129 individuals, including the former president — to the Federal Police, PGR, and STF.

item.title: Relatório da CPI do INSS é rejeitado; saiba como votaram os parlamentares
item.description: The INSS CPI rejected the final report drafted by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), which called for the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), banker Daniel Vorcaro, Senator Weverton Rocha (PDT-MA), Deputy Euclydes Pettersen (Republicanos-MG), Deputy Gorete Pereira (MDB-CE), and 211 others. The vote took place in the early hours of Saturday, the 28th, in Brasília, with participation from deputies and senators.

item.title: CPMI do INSS termina sob inércia investigativa, mas com desgaste político para Lula
item.description: The INSS CPMI concluded without new evidence but inflicted significant political damage on President Lula by linking his son to corruption suspicions, reinforcing Bolsonarist narratives and reviving corruption as Brazilians' top concern — surpassing crime and inflation, per AtlasIntel/Bloomberg. Though hampered by judicial rulings blocking evidence gathering, the commission fulfilled its role as an electoral platform, aiding the opposition on social media and undermining the PT’s ethical discourse ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: CPMI do INSS rejeita relatório que pedia indiciamento de ‘Lulinha’
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected, by a vote of 19 to 12, the report by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar calling for the indictment of 216 individuals, including Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Lula. The decision followed the STF’s overturning of Minister André Mendonça’s order to extend the CPMI. Committee President Senator Carlos Viana adjourned the meeting without allowing a vote on an alternative report that included names such as Jair Bolsonaro and Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: CPMI do INSS rejeita relatório que indiciava e pedia prisão de Lulinha
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected the final report drafted by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), which called for the arrest of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Lula, and the indictment of 216 individuals. The decision was made in the early hours of today, suggesting a lack of consensus or insufficient evidence to support the allegations.

item.title: CPMI do INSS rejeita relatório final de Alfredo Gaspar por 19 a 12
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected the final report by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar by a vote of 19 to 12, concluding seven months of work without an official opinion. The report included indictment requests against Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), linked to lobbyist 'Careca do INSS', for crimes such as criminal organization and money laundering. An alternative report by Deputy Paulo Pimenta, calling for the indictment of Jair and Flávio Bolsonaro, was also dismissed by CPMI President Carlos Viana.

item.title: CPMI do INSS rejeita relatório que pedia indiciamento de 'Lulinha' e ex-ministros de Lula e Bolsonaro
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) rejected its final report recommending the indictment of over 200 individuals, including President Lula’s son Fábio Luís Lula da Silva ('Lulinha'), former ministers, and lawmakers. The report was voted down 19-12, leaving the committee without an approved document before its deadline. The committee president stated copies of the report would still be sent to the STF and PGR, despite the rejection.

item.title: Parlamentares denunciam relator da CPI do INSS à PF por estupro
item.description: Federal deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) and senator Soraya Thronicke (Podemos-MS) accused Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), the rapporteur of the INSS CPI, of rape and attempted bribery involving a 13-year-old minor in Alagoas nine years ago. Gaspar denied the allegations, claiming they refer to a cousin and are false attempts to derail the CPI’s investigations. The incident caused turmoil in the committee, including verbal exchanges and a request to remove an offensive term from the records.

item.title: Tebet defende que Alckmin continue como vice-presidente
item.description: Simone Tebet (PSB) defended that Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) should remain as vice-president on Lula's (PT) 2026 ticket, stating 'a winning team doesn't change'. She also announced her Senate candidacy on the governing coalition ticket, while Alckmin left open the possibility of running for Senate.

item.title: Tebet lança candidatura ao Senado e critica governo de SP: "Ingrato"
item.description: Simone Tebet, Minister of Planning and Budget, formalized her affiliation with the PSB and launched her pre-candidacy for the São Paulo Senate, criticizing Governor Tarcísio de Freitas as 'absolutely ungrateful' for not acknowledging the federal government's role in securing external financing for the state. She emphasized that over US$4.5 billion in international financing, guaranteed by the Union, was made possible through her engagement with President Lula, and that the state benefits from these funds without showing gratitude.

item.title: Caiado x Leite: o que dizem os números da Quaest sobre a disputa nas prévias do PSD
item.description: Governors Ronaldo Caiado (GO) and Eduardo Leite (RS) show similar performance in the first round of the PSD’s 2026 presidential primaries, with 4% and 3% voting intentions, respectively. In a hypothetical second-round runoff against Lula, Caiado performs better with 32% support versus Leite’s 26%, though both trail the incumbent president. Caiado has greater vote potential and an edge on public security, while Leite is considered more moderate.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro pede livre acesso dos filhos à domiciliar
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro requested unrestricted access for his children to the residence where he is serving house arrest, as ordered by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes. The request aims to allow his heirs to visit him without restrictions during the enforcement of the precautionary measure.

item.title: 'Sempre vou consultar meu pai, mas o candidato sou eu', diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL's presidential candidate for 2026, stated during his third visit to the U.S. that while he regularly consults his father Jair Bolsonaro, the final decision in the election is his own. He ruled out the possibility that his father, currently under house arrest, would hold any position in his potential administration.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleições indiretas para governador do Rio e mantém presidente do TJ-RJ no cargo
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect elections for governor of Rio de Janeiro and ordered that the president of the TJ-RJ, Justice Ricardo Couto de Castro, remain in office until the Court rules on the case. The decision was requested as a highlight in ADI 7942, concerning electoral rules in the state, and should be ratified by the full Court, preferably in person.

item.title: STF: Zanin suspende eleição indireta no RJ; caso vai a plenário presencial
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for the temporary gubernatorial mandate in Rio de Janeiro and requested a highlight for the case to be judged in the Court's physical plenary. The decision follows the STF's prior formation of a majority in favor of indirect and secret elections in another process. The judgment will be restarted, with the date to be set by Court President Minister Edson Fachin.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleição indireta para mandato-tampão no RJ
item.description: STF Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for the temporary mandate in Rio de Janeiro, keeping the president of the state’s Court of Justice in office until the final ruling. He also requested that the case on election rules be heard in the physical plenary, jointly with another proceeding, and denounced Claudio Castro’s resignation as a fraudulent maneuver to evade punishment and manipulate the electoral process. The decision responds to a petition by the PSD, Eduardo Paes’s party, which challenged the TSE’s order to make the supplementary election indirect.

item.title: Por que Zanin suspendeu eleição indireta para governador do Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, keeping Rio’s Court of Justice President Ricardo Couto in office pending final judgment. The decision supports a PSD petition led by Eduardo Paes calling for a direct vote, following Zanin’s withdrawal of his prior vote in favor of indirect election. The case now proceeds to the STF’s physical plenary, as Rio faces a leadership vacuum after Castro’s resignation one day before his TSE conviction.

item.title: Tebet diz que Nunes é “deselegante” ao chamá-la de “marionete de Lula”
item.description: Minister Simone Tebet criticized São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes as 'absolutely inelegant' after he called her a 'Lula puppet' during her PSB affiliation. She said she holds affection for Nunes but found the remark offensive to Brazilian women. Tebet affirmed her respect for President Lula and Vice President Alckmin, stating she is running for Senate in 2026 at their request, after leaving the MDB after 29 years.

item.title: Nova biografia de Lula mantém tom acrítico e tem autor como personagem oculto
item.description: A new biography of Lula is criticized for maintaining an uncritical tone and for having the author as a hidden character in the narrative, raising concerns about its impartiality. The full content of the article could not be accessed due to the site's robots.txt restrictions.

item.title: Zanin suspende eleições indiretas para governador do Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin suspended the indirect election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, keeping the state's Court of Justice president, Ricardo Couto de Castro, in office until an in-person ruling. The decision follows a new PSD action challenging the TSE's authorization for indirect elections, with Zanin advocating for joint discussion with a separate case calling for direct elections. While the Court majority previously voted for a secret indirect ballot, Zanin and three others support direct voting.

item.title: Zanin interrompe julgamento, suspende nova eleição e mantém governador interino no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin withdrew his vote in the case concerning new gubernatorial elections in Rio de Janeiro and issued an injunction suspending the indirect election for the post. The decision keeps the interim governor in office pending further judicial resolution.

item.title: Rodrigo Bacellar é preso novamente: os tentáculos do Comando Vermelho na política do Rio, segundo investigações
item.description: Rodrigo Bacellar, former president of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislature (Alerj), was arrested again in Operation Unha e Carne III on suspicion of leaking confidential information and obstructing investigations tied to criminal organizations in Rio. The arrest followed the annulment of his mandate by the TSE and the loss of privileged jurisdiction, despite his lawyers claiming he had fully complied with all precautionary measures. Bacellar had previously been arrested in December and released after the Alerj revoked the detention.

item.title: O que Moraes disse ao decretar a nova prisão de Rodrigo Bacellar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes ordered a new preventive detention for former state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar (União) after he lost his parliamentary mandate by TSE ruling, citing the need to maintain public order and evidence of coordinated actions to obstruct investigations. Bacellar was denounced by the PGR for obstructing an investigation involving an armed criminal organization and public agent participation.
